project.name: Eclipse
project.description: Local-first AI workspace configuration manager with a library-first architecture that stores agents, skills, and rules, renders them with Jinja2, and exports tool-specific outputs for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Windsurf, and OpenCode.

nodes.name: Eclipse
nodes.summary: Local-first AI workspace configuration manager with a library-first architecture that stores agents, skills, and MCP/server definitions, renders them with Jinja2, and exports tool-specific outputs for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Windsurf, and OpenCode.

nodes.name: .DS_Store
nodes.summary: macOS Finder metadata file at the repository root; not part of application logic.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Repository agent policy defining architectural constraints such as library-first storage, pure exporters, path safety, atomic persistence, and type-safe handling of library items.

nodes.name: Eclipse.spec
nodes.summary: PyInstaller specification for packaging the GUI application as a macOS app bundle, including templates and hidden imports for exporters and importers.

nodes.name: Icon
nodes.summary: Icon metadata file used by macOS packaging or Finder presentation; contains no source logic.

nodes.name: LICENSE
nodes.summary: MIT license text defining the legal terms for use, modification, and distribution of Eclipse.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Top-level project documentation describing Eclipse's library-first architecture, quick start workflow, supported export targets, and macOS installation notes.

nodes.name: Eclipse.icns
nodes.summary: Application icon asset bundled into the packaged macOS application.

nodes.name: .DS_Store
nodes.summary: macOS Finder metadata file in the exports directory; unrelated to runtime behavior.

nodes.name: .DS_Store
nodes.summary: macOS Finder metadata file in the library directory; unrelated to application behavior.

nodes.name: instructions.md
nodes.summary: Instruction content for the Python Bug Fixer agent, emphasizing root-cause debugging, minimal diffs, and evidence-based test verification.

nodes.name: manifest.json
nodes.summary: Manifest describing the Python Bug Fixer library agent, its supported targets, render strategies, required tools, and associated instruction file.

nodes.name: Python Bug Fixer
nodes.summary: Workspace-scoped reusable agent definition for fixing Python bugs with minimal side effects across supported AI coding tools.

nodes.name: manifest.json
nodes.summary: Manifest describing the GitHub MCP server library item, including supported targets, runtime requirements, and the server configuration source file.

nodes.name: GitHub MCP
nodes.summary: Global MCP server definition for GitHub-backed repository operations distributed as configuration fragments for multiple coding tools.

nodes.name: server.json
nodes.summary: Concrete MCP server transport and command configuration referenced by the GitHub MCP manifest.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill instructions for minimally fixing failing Python tests by diagnosing failures, applying focused corrections, and rerunning affected tests.

nodes.name: manifest.json
nodes.summary: Manifest describing the Python Test Fix skill, including supported targets, tool requirements, and its entry instruction file.

nodes.name: Python Test Fix
nodes.summary: Workspace-scoped reusable skill that guides AI tools to diagnose and repair failing Python tests with minimal intervention.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml
nodes.summary: Python project manifest defining package metadata, dependencies, CLI entry points, build backend, setuptools package discovery, and Ruff configuration.

nodes.name: __main__.py
nodes.summary: Module entry point that delegates python -m agentbox execution to the main application launcher.

nodes.name: app.py
nodes.summary: Main GUI application controller that initializes the library workspace, coordinates rendering, import/export operations, validation, and user interactions through the main window.

nodes.name: EclipseApp
nodes.summary: QMainWindow-based application controller that owns library state, renderer setup, UI wiring, preview generation, exporting, deletion, and import workflows.

nodes.name: EclipseApp.__init__
nodes.summary: Initializes the main window, ensures library directory structure exists, constructs the template renderer and UI, and triggers the initial library load.

nodes.name: EclipseApp.reload_library
nodes.summary: Scans library items from disk, validates them, updates status text, and repopulates the library panel; reports fatal load errors through a message box.

nodes.name: EclipseApp.on_item_selected
nodes.summary: Stores the selected library item, updates status text, and refreshes the preview for the current export target.

nodes.name: EclipseApp.update_preview
nodes.summary: Performs a dry-run template render for the selected item and active target to populate the preview panel without writing export files.

nodes.name: EclipseApp.on_export_selected
nodes.summary: Validates the selected item, instantiates the target-specific exporter, writes exported files to the per-item export directory, and optionally opens the folder in Finder.

nodes.name: EclipseApp.on_item_delete_requested
nodes.summary: Confirms and performs permanent deletion of a library item, then reloads the library view.

nodes.name: EclipseApp.on_github_import
nodes.summary: Starts a GitHub import, handles warning-only results, and forwards successful detections for user confirmation and saving.

nodes.name: EclipseApp._process_import_result
nodes.summary: Builds an item-detection summary dialog and, when approved, saves each detected import result into the local library before reloading the UI.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Exporter package registry that imports all concrete exporters and maps target names to their implementation classes.

nodes.name: EXPORTER_CLASSES
nodes.summary: Central mapping from export target identifiers to concrete exporter classes used by the application controller.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Abstract exporter base module defining the shared exporter interface and helper methods for writing files and reading sidecar content.

nodes.name: BaseExporter
nodes.summary: Common exporter superclass that stores the template renderer, defines the export contract, and provides reusable file IO helpers.

nodes.name: BaseExporter.__init__
nodes.summary: Captures the shared template renderer used by concrete exporters.

nodes.name: BaseExporter.export_item
nodes.summary: Abstract export operation that concrete exporters implement to write target-specific file layouts for a library item.

nodes.name: BaseExporter.write_text
nodes.summary: Helper that ensures parent directories exist, normalizes trailing newlines, and writes UTF-8 text to disk.

nodes.name: BaseExporter._read_sidecar
nodes.summary: Reads optional sidecar content from a library item's root directory when the referenced file exists.

nodes.name: claude_code.py
nodes.summary: Claude Code exporter that writes docs, agents, MCP server configs, and skill files into the directory structure expected by Claude Code.

nodes.name: ClaudeCodeExporter
nodes.summary: Concrete exporter for Claude Code that renders or copies library items into .claude-specific output paths.

nodes.name: ClaudeCodeExporter.export_item
nodes.summary: Exports docs, agents, MCP definitions, and skill files to Claude Code's expected layout using target-aware render rules.

nodes.name: ClaudeCodeExporter._render_as_for_target
nodes.summary: Finds the render mode declared for a specific target in a library item's render metadata.

nodes.name: ClaudeCodeExporter._has_render
nodes.summary: Checks whether a library item declares a particular render mode for the specified target.

nodes.name: codex.py
nodes.summary: Codex exporter that renders docs, agent files, skills, resources, and MCP definitions into Codex-compatible locations.

nodes.name: CodexExporter
nodes.summary: Concrete exporter for Codex that combines rendered metadata with copied skill resources under the .codex directory.

nodes.name: CodexExporter.export_item
nodes.summary: Exports documentation, agent definitions, skills with resources, and MCP server configs to Codex-specific output paths.

nodes.name: cursor.py
nodes.summary: Cursor exporter that writes AGENTS documentation and rule files, honoring render output path overrides when present.

nodes.name: CursorExporter
nodes.summary: Concrete exporter for Cursor that maps docs, agents, and rules into Cursor-compatible files.

nodes.name: CursorExporter.export_item
nodes.summary: Exports AGENTS content for docs or agents and emits rule files to default or overridden Cursor rule paths.

nodes.name: opencode.py
nodes.summary: Provisional OpenCode exporter that writes docs, agent files, and MCP configs to currently assumed directory names.

nodes.name: OpenCodeExporter
nodes.summary: Concrete exporter for OpenCode with provisional path conventions for agent and MCP outputs.

nodes.name: OpenCodeExporter.export_item
nodes.summary: Exports documentation, agent definitions, and MCP server configs using provisional OpenCode file layout assumptions.

nodes.name: windsurf.py
nodes.summary: Windsurf exporter that emits AGENTS docs, rules, workflows, and MCP server definitions into Windsurf-specific directories.

nodes.name: WindsurfExporter
nodes.summary: Concrete exporter for Windsurf that renders multiple library item types into .windsurf folders.

nodes.name: WindsurfExporter.export_item
nodes.summary: Exports docs or agents as AGENTS.md plus rules, workflows, and MCP configs into Windsurf-specific output locations.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/importers/__init__.py
nodes.summary: Re-exports importer models and import workflow helpers as the public importers package API.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/importers/base.py
nodes.summary: Re-exports import-related dataclasses from the shared models module for importer-local consumption.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/importers/github_url.py
nodes.summary: Clones a GitHub repository into a temporary directory, scans it for supported items, and reports warnings on failure.

nodes.name: handle_github_import
nodes.summary: Normalizes GitHub URLs, performs a shallow git clone into a temp folder, then delegates content detection to scan_path.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/importers/local_drop.py
nodes.summary: Handles local file or directory imports by validating the dropped path and scanning it for supported items.

nodes.name: handle_local_drop
nodes.summary: Resolves a dropped path, returns a warning result for missing paths, and otherwise delegates to scan_path.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/importers/save_to_library.py
nodes.summary: Transforms detected imported content into Eclipse library folders, copies sidecar files, builds manifests, and persists them.

nodes.name: slugify
nodes.summary: Converts arbitrary names into filesystem-safe library IDs using lowercase normalization and restricted characters.

nodes.name: save_detected_to_library
nodes.summary: Creates a destination folder for a detected item, copies canonical content files by type, synthesizes a LibraryItem manifest, and saves it to disk.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/importers/scanner.py
nodes.summary: Walks directories, skips junk folders, heuristically detects supported library item types, and returns structured import results.

nodes.name: _is_under_named_dir
nodes.summary: Checks whether a path contains a named directory segment; defined as a utility but unused in this file.

nodes.name: _looks_like_mcp_json
nodes.summary: Reads a JSON file and uses command, args, and transport keys to classify it as an MCP server definition.

nodes.name: scan_path
nodes.summary: Recursively scans a root path with directory pruning, detects agents, skills, docs, rules, workflows, and MCP JSON files, and returns sorted detection results.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/main.py
nodes.summary: Bootstraps the Qt application, creates the top-level Eclipse app controller, shows the main window, and starts the event loop.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Creates a QApplication, sets app metadata, instantiates EclipseApp, displays it, and exits with the Qt event loop result.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/models.py
nodes.summary: Defines core library, rendering, and importer dataclasses plus literal type aliases used across storage, validation, rendering, and import flows.

nodes.name: RenderTarget
nodes.summary: Describes how a library item should render for a specific export target, including output path overrides.

nodes.name: Requirements
nodes.summary: Captures required shell commands and environment variables for an item.

nodes.name: Metadata
nodes.summary: Tracks author and created/updated timestamps for persisted items.

nodes.name: LibraryItem
nodes.summary: Canonical neutral representation of a library item that flows through storage, validation, rendering, and export layers.

nodes.name: DetectedItem
nodes.summary: Represents a candidate item discovered during import scanning before it is saved into the library.

nodes.name: ImportResult
nodes.summary: Carries detected import candidates and warnings produced by a local or GitHub scan.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/paths.py
nodes.summary: Provides canonical filesystem locations for project roots, library storage, exports, templates, backups, and per-target export directories.

nodes.name: project_root
nodes.summary: Computes the repository root by walking two directories up from paths.py.

nodes.name: library_root
nodes.summary: Returns the canonical library directory beneath the project root.

nodes.name: exports_root
nodes.summary: Returns the disposable exports output directory beneath the project root.

nodes.name: templates_root
nodes.summary: Returns the package-local Jinja2 templates directory.

nodes.name: backups_root
nodes.summary: Returns the backups directory used for optional library snapshots.

nodes.name: target_export_dir
nodes.summary: Builds the export output directory path for a specific target tool.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/render.py
nodes.summary: Wraps a Jinja2 environment to render library items into target-specific output formats with shared context.

nodes.name: TemplateRenderer
nodes.summary: Creates and manages a Jinja2 environment for rendering item templates and checking template availability.

nodes.name: TemplateRenderer.__init__
nodes.summary: Initializes the template renderer with a file-system loader and strict Jinja2 undefined handling.

nodes.name: TemplateRenderer.render
nodes.summary: Loads a template, constructs a normalized context from a LibraryItem, merges optional extras, and returns newline-normalized rendered text.

nodes.name: TemplateRenderer.template_exists
nodes.summary: Checks whether a template is available without propagating TemplateNotFound.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/storage.py
nodes.summary: Loads, saves, scans, renames, and deletes library items and their manifests on disk.

nodes.name: TYPE_DIRS
nodes.summary: Maps canonical item types to their on-disk library directory names.

nodes.name: load_manifest
nodes.summary: Deserializes a manifest JSON file into a fully constructed LibraryItem with typed nested metadata and requirements.

nodes.name: save_manifest
nodes.summary: Serializes a LibraryItem to manifest.json using atomic tmp-file replacement and timestamp refresh.

nodes.name: scan_library
nodes.summary: Finds every manifest.json under the library root, loads items, warns on failures, and returns sorted results.

nodes.name: item_manifest_path
nodes.summary: Resolves an item's manifest path from its root directory and fails if the item was not loaded from disk.

nodes.name: save_item
nodes.summary: Persists updates for an existing item by saving to its manifest path.

nodes.name: delete_item
nodes.summary: Moves an item's directory to the macOS Trash using Finder via AppleScript.

nodes.name: rename_item_dir
nodes.summary: Renames an item's library directory, updates its id and root_dir, and saves the updated manifest.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/templates/AGENTS.md.j2
nodes.summary: Renders AGENTS.md output with purpose, optional instructions, constraints, invocation text, required tools, and tags.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/templates/CLAUDE.md.j2
nodes.summary: Renders CLAUDE.md output combining description or content with optional behavior, rules, commands, and environment requirements.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/templates/README.md.j2
nodes.summary: Renders README content with description, overview, required tools, environment variables, tags, and Eclipse attribution footer.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/templates/claude_agent.md.j2
nodes.summary: Renders a Claude-targeted agent file from instructions or description plus optional constraints and invocation text.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/templates/codex_agent.md.j2
nodes.summary: Renders a Codex-targeted agent file with instructions, constraints, and required tools.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/templates/cursor_rule.mdc.j2
nodes.summary: Renders Cursor rule files with frontmatter metadata and optional rule constraints.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/templates/mcp_server.json.j2
nodes.summary: Renders JSON configuration fragments for MCP servers using item identity, transport, command, args, and environment data.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/templates/windsurf_workflow.md.j2
nodes.summary: Renders Windsurf workflow markdown with instructions, numbered steps, and optional notes.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/ui/main_window.py
nodes.summary: Defines the main Qt widgets for import controls, library list management, item inspection, preview display, and overall window layout.

nodes.name: ImportBar
nodes.summary: Top-bar widget for GitHub URL input and drag-and-drop affordance, emitting import requests.

nodes.name: ImportBar.__init__
nodes.summary: Builds URL input, import button, and drop-zone label UI for import actions.

nodes.name: ImportBar._on_click
nodes.summary: Emits the entered GitHub URL when the import button is clicked and the input is non-empty.

nodes.name: LibraryListPanel
nodes.summary: Left-side library panel that lists items and offers refresh, reveal, and delete actions via the app controller.

nodes.name: LibraryListPanel.__init__
nodes.summary: Builds the library list widget and management toolbar.

nodes.name: LibraryListPanel.populate
nodes.summary: Clears and repopulates the list widget with library item names and types.

nodes.name: LibraryListPanel._on_selection
nodes.summary: Forwards the currently selected item to the application controller.

nodes.name: LibraryListPanel._on_reveal
nodes.summary: Opens the selected item's root directory in the system file browser.

nodes.name: LibraryListPanel._on_delete
nodes.summary: Delegates deletion of the selected item to the application controller.

nodes.name: LibraryListPanel.current_item
nodes.summary: Returns the currently selected LibraryItem by row index if the panel's backing item list is available.

nodes.name: EditorPanel
nodes.summary: Middle panel that shows a pretty-printed JSON view of the selected library item's manifest data.

nodes.name: EditorPanel.__init__
nodes.summary: Builds the read-only manifest detail viewer UI.

nodes.name: EditorPanel.show_item
nodes.summary: Serializes a LibraryItem to formatted JSON text and displays it in the editor area.

nodes.name: PreviewPanel
nodes.summary: Right-side panel that shows rendered export preview text.

nodes.name: PreviewPanel.__init__
nodes.summary: Builds the read-only preview text area UI.

nodes.name: PreviewPanel.set_content
nodes.summary: Updates the preview area with rendered export content.

nodes.name: MainWindowWidget
nodes.summary: Composes the import bar, target toolbar, three-pane library/editor/preview layout, and status bar for the main UI.

nodes.name: MainWindowWidget.__init__
nodes.summary: Stores the application controller reference and initializes the window UI.

nodes.name: MainWindowWidget.setup_ui
nodes.summary: Creates the import bar, target selector, export button, split panels, and status bar, and applies the global stylesheet.

nodes.name: MainWindowWidget.on_item_selected
nodes.summary: Shows the selected item in the editor panel and notifies the app controller.

nodes.name: MainWindowWidget.set_status
nodes.summary: Displays a temporary status message in the window status bar.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/ui/styles.py
nodes.summary: Defines the STELLAR_STYLE Qt stylesheet string used to theme the main application interface.

nodes.name: STELLAR_STYLE
nodes.summary: Dark, purple-accented Qt stylesheet covering widgets, lists, toolbars, status bar, inputs, buttons, labels, and drop zones.

nodes.name: src/agentbox/validators.py
nodes.summary: Validates library items and whole libraries for identifier format, type and target correctness, required data keys, and sidecar file presence.

nodes.name: _ID_RE
nodes.summary: Regular expression enforcing valid lowercase item identifiers.

nodes.name: VALID_ITEM_TYPES
nodes.summary: Frozen set of canonical library item types accepted by validation.

nodes.name: VALID_TARGETS
nodes.summary: Frozen set of supported export targets and the all wildcard.

nodes.name: VALID_RENDER_AS
nodes.summary: Frozen set of allowed render_as values for render target declarations.

nodes.name: REQUIRED_DATA_KEYS
nodes.summary: Maps item types to required keys that must be present in each item's data payload.

nodes.name: validate_item
nodes.summary: Runs single-item validation for id, name, type, targets, render mappings, required data keys, and sidecar existence.

nodes.name: _validate_sidecars
nodes.summary: Verifies that required content sidecar files exist on disk for each persisted item type.

nodes.name: validate_library
nodes.summary: Validates an entire library, including duplicate ID detection and prefixed per-item errors.

layers.name: Foundation Assets & Project Governance
layers.description: Repository-level documentation, packaging metadata, legal files, and bundled binary assets that define how the project is built, distributed, and understood but do not implement runtime behavior directly.

layers.name: Library Content & Workspace Data
layers.description: The user/library-facing content stored on disk: starter agents, skills, MCP definitions, and output directories that represent the managed workspace state rather than application code.

layers.name: Core Domain Model & Path Mapping
layers.description: Shared low-level runtime primitives that define the shape of library items and the canonical filesystem locations used by every higher layer.

layers.name: Core Services: Validation, Storage & Rendering
layers.description: Reusable application services that validate item shape, load/save library content, and render tool-specific text from templates. These services implement the business rules of the library-first system without owning UI concerns.

layers.name: Integration Adapters: Import & Export
layers.description: Boundary adapters that translate between Eclipse's internal library representation and external sources or target tools. Importers ingest outside content into the library; exporters project library items into tool-specific outputs.

layers.name: Application Orchestration & UI
layers.description: Top-level desktop runtime layer that wires services and adapters into PySide6 event handling, window presentation, selection/edit flows, import/export actions, and application startup.

layers.name: Quality & Verification
layers.description: Testing and static-quality layer declared by project tooling and repository layout. It supports the architecture rather than participating in runtime execution.

tour.title: 1. Start at the module entry point
tour.description: This matches the README quick-start exactly and gives you the cleanest starting point for understanding runtime flow.

tour.title: 2. See how the desktop app boots
tour.description: This is the bridge from Python packaging into the actual GUI-driven orchestration layer.

tour.title: 3. Understand the main orchestration hub
tour.description: If the README says Eclipse is Library → Renderer → Exporters, this file is where those pieces are composed into a working product.

tour.title: 4. Map the visible product surface
tour.description: This file shows how the product presents the README architecture to a user: browse the library, inspect an item, preview its rendered form, then export it to a target tool.

tour.title: 5. Learn the neutral core model and filesystem contract
tour.description: Everything else depends on this neutrality. Importers produce these models, validators inspect them, the renderer consumes them, and exporters translate them for outside tools.

tour.title: 6. Follow the local-first library persistence story
tour.description: This is the concrete implementation of the README's library tier and the reason the app is local-first rather than database-backed or remote-first.

tour.title: 7. See how Eclipse protects library integrity
tour.description: The app validates both on initial load and before export, which keeps malformed library content from propagating into rendered tool outputs.

tour.title: 8. Understand the Jinja2 rendering engine
tour.description: This is the middle tier in the README architecture: the library stays tool-agnostic, and the templates translate it into each tool's expected format.

tour.title: 9. Compare the tool-specific export adapters
tour.description: This is the final tier of the README architecture. The same library item can be expressed differently depending on whether the target is Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Windsurf, or OpenCode.

tour.title: 10. Trace how outside content becomes library items
tour.description: This closes the library-first loop. Eclipse is not only an exporter; it is also a normalizer that absorbs externally authored workspace assets into a single internal representation.

tour.title: 11. Inspect the bundled library as example domain data
tour.description: Reading these files makes the abstract model concrete and shows what authors are expected to place in the library.

tour.title: 12. Finish with packaging and distribution metadata
tour.description: These files connect the architecture to actual developer and end-user workflows: editable installs, CLI scripts, dependencies, and a PyInstaller GUI bundle.

tour.title: 13. Read the repository's intended architecture in prose
tour.description: End your tour here to compare the code you just traced with the explicit design claims the project makes about itself.
